dimensions.dimension_name: Emotional vs Rational Advertising Appeal Sensitivity
dimensions.what_it_captures: Whether the person is more persuaded by emotional storytelling or logical facts in marketing messages, which affects how they internalize brand narratives.
dimensions.concept_test_impact: Variation would cause different responses: emotional-oriented twins prefer product concepts highlighting social benefits or feelings, while rational-oriented twins favor concepts with clinical proof or ingredient transparency.
dimensions.ad_test_impact: Emotional twins engage more with ads using narratives of social acceptance or joy; rational twins respond better to ads showcasing expert endorsements or data-driven claims.
dimensions.what_we_can_infer: From Q8, they care about others' perceptions (emotional cue), but from Q17, they trust dermatologists (rational cue), leaving their dominant response style uncertain.
dimensions.example_variation: Twin A (emotional) sees a body wash ad depicting friendship and positive feedback, leading to high purchase intent; Twin B (rational) sees the same ad but dismisses it, preferring an ad with lab test results.

dimensions.dimension_name: Visual vs Textual Content Processing Style
dimensions.what_it_captures: Preference for absorbing information through visual elements (images, videos) versus written text in marketing materials, influencing engagement depth.
dimensions.concept_test_impact: Visual processors resonate with concepts featuring bold packaging imagery or infographics; textual processors prefer detailed descriptions, ingredient lists, and written benefits.
dimensions.ad_test_impact: Visual twins click on video or image-heavy social media ads; textual twins engage more with blog-style ads or text-based reviews in campaigns.
dimensions.what_we_can_infer: Q41 indicates playful packaging preference, hinting at visual appeal, but no questions address ad content format, so processing style remains unclear.
dimensions.example_variation: Twin A (visual) is captivated by an Instagram ad with vibrant colors and minimal text, leading to exploration; Twin B (textual) ignores it, favoring a Facebook ad with extensive product details.

dimensions.dimension_name: Aspirational vs Relatable Brand Positioning Response
dimensions.what_it_captures: How well the person connects with marketing that depicts ideal, upgraded lifestyles versus everyday, authentic scenarios, affecting brand affinity.
dimensions.concept_test_impact: Aspirational twins are drawn to concepts positioned as premium or transformative; relatable twins prefer concepts emphasizing practicality, value, and real-user scenarios.
dimensions.ad_test_impact: Aspirational twins engage with ads showing luxurious lifestyles or celebrity endorsements; relatable twins respond to ads featuring ordinary people or honest testimonials.
dimensions.what_we_can_infer: As a student with energetic personality (Q6) and concern for perception (Q8), they might aspire, but income and price sensitivity (Q12, Q21) suggest relatability, creating ambiguity.
dimensions.example_variation: Twin A (aspirational) loves a body wash concept framed as a luxury spa experience, willing to pay more; Twin B (relatable) rejects it, opting for a concept highlighting everyday freshness at low cost.

dimensions.dimension_name: Impulse vs Deliberate Purchase Decision Making
dimensions.what_it_captures: Tendency to make quick, unplanned purchases based on immediate cues versus engaging in careful research and comparison before buying.
dimensions.concept_test_impact: Impulse twins respond positively to concepts with limited-time offers or flashy displays; deliberate twins require comprehensive information, comparisons, and time to evaluate.
dimensions.ad_test_impact: Impulse twins click on ads with urgency triggers (e.g., 'buy now' discounts); deliberate twins ignore such ads, seeking out reviews or detailed content first.
dimensions.what_we_can_infer: Q23 shows discount triggered a switch, suggesting impulse, but Q19 involves browsing online, indicating deliberation, so the dominant tendency is not definitive.
dimensions.example_variation: Twin A (impulse) sees a quick commerce ad for a body wash on sale and buys immediately; Twin B (deliberate) sees the same ad, bookmarks it, and compares prices across platforms before deciding.

dimensions.what_it_captures: How current mood, occasion, or environmental context (e.g., location, time of day) shapes openness to advertising messages and product concepts.
dimensions.concept_test_impact: Mood-sensitive twins may reject concepts when stressed but embrace them when relaxed; context-consistent twins evaluate concepts uniformly regardless of situation.
dimensions.ad_test_impact: Mood-sensitive twins ignore ads seen during busy work hours but engage with them during leisure; context-consistent twins maintain steady engagement across contexts.
dimensions.what_we_can_infer: No questions address mood or context effects; Q31 mentions bathing as a small pleasure, but this doesn't extend to ad receptivity, leaving this dimension entirely uncaptured.
dimensions.example_variation: Twin A (mood-sensitive) sees a body wash ad while stressed and scrolls past; Twin B (context-consistent) sees the same ad and evaluates it based on content, regardless of mood.
